title: Voice Note: Airport at 4am
content:
Transcription of voice memo recorded at gate B14, 4:17am.

I keep coming back to airports at strange hours. There's something about them — the way they're full of people who are all, technically, in the same moment of leaving. Nobody is home. Nobody has arrived yet. We're all suspended.

The cleaning crew is mopping the floors in the far end of the terminal. The sound travels differently at this hour. There's a woman asleep across three seats with her coat over her face, which seems like an act of radical self-trust.

I've been thinking about what it means to keep choosing movement. Every time I book a flight I think: this is running. But then I get somewhere and it doesn't feel like running. It feels like — reaching? Or maybe like throwing myself at the world and seeing if it catches me.

The coffee from this machine is terrible but I'm holding it like it's the last warm thing on earth.

I think I need to stop moving for a while. Not today. But soon.

Okay the gate agent is glaring at me. I should stop talking into my phone. I look insane.
